hook: ChatGPT keeps giving you 5-step frameworks when you just needed a yes or no.
caption:
ChatGPT keeps giving you 5-step frameworks when you just needed a yes or no.

This isn't a you problem. It's a calibration problem.

AI defaults to the most "helpful-looking" response — which usually means longer, more structured, more cautious. Because that's what works for the average person asking that question.

But you're not the average person asking that question.

Maybe you're a fast processor who needs the conclusion first, context second. Maybe you hate bullet points and think better in plain paragraphs. Maybe you're a devil's advocate thinker who needs the counterargument, not the cheerleading.

AI doesn't know any of that. So it guesses. And it guesses generic.

The fix isn't longer prompts. It's not magic words.

It's giving AI a profile of how YOU think — so it stops pattern-matching to the average user and starts responding to you.

That's what we built bootfile.ai for.

Your thinking style. Your communication preferences. Your blind spots and how you like to be challenged. Packaged into one instruction profile you paste into any AI tool.

Same AI. Completely different experience.
